response: Phone begins with the total number of games played, which is twelve. Phone calculates the number of wins and losses by using the ratio of two to one. Phone determines that the team won eight games and lost four. Phone uses the average points per game to find the total points scored in wins. Phone multiplies the number of wins by the average points in those games. Phone finds that the team scored a total of two hundred twenty-four points in victories. Phone then calculates the total points scored in losses by multiplying the number of losses by the average points in those games. Phone discovers that the team scored fifty-six points in their losses. Phone adds the points from wins and losses to determine the total points scored for the season. Phone concludes that Samford scored two hundred eighty points throughout the season.
